provenance.source_corpus: Nitrogen recovery in planted seedlings, competing vegetation, and soil in response to fertilization on a boreal mine reclamation site

source_spans.text: controlled-release fertilizer (CRF) applications
immediately available fertilizer (IAF) applications
Seedlings receiving CRF exhibited increased diameter and organ dry mass
Seedlings receiving CRF exhibited increased diameter and organ dry mass
Both IAF and CRF promoted comparable increases in seedling N status
Both fertilization treatments increased N concentration and content of seedlings
Both fertilization treatments increased N concentration and content of seedlings
Overall recovery of applied 15N was low and varied significantly by fertilizer treatment
soils surrounding IAF and, to a lesser extent, CRF seedlings had the greatest concentration of applied 15N
Neither IAF nor CRF affected vegetation cover or dry mass
